title: Add non-transfer token ledger diagnostics to confirm mainnet ledger IDs are wired correctly

requirements.text: Confirm ckBTC, ckETH, and ckUSDC are configured with the correct Internet Computer mainnet ledger canister IDs by adding a token configuration self-check that compares the configured IDs in `frontend/src/config/tokens.ts` against the expected mainnet ledger IDs and reports any mismatch.

quotes: check and confirm whether the correct mainnet ledger IDs confirm everything is wired up properly without sending any real ckBTC, ckETH, or ckUSDC.

acceptanceCriteria: A single function (or equivalent) exists that returns, per token (ckBTC/ckETH/ckUSDC), the configured ledger canister ID, the expected mainnet ledger canister ID, and a boolean indicating whether they match exactly.
When a token is missing a ledger canister ID, the self-check reports it as not configured and does not throw.
The expected mainnet ledger canister IDs used for the comparison are: ckBTC = mxzaz-hqaaa-aaaar-qaada-cai; ckETH = ss2fx-dyaaa-aaaar-qacoq-cai; ckUSDC = xevnm-gaaaa-aaaar-qafnq-cai.

requirements.text: Add a read-only “Token Ledger Diagnostics” UI on the Dashboard that lets the user verify wiring without any transfers by displaying (for each token) the configured ledger canister ID, whether it matches the expected mainnet ID, and a copy-to-clipboard JSON diagnostic report.

quotes: confirm everything is wired up properly without sending any real ckBTC, ckETH, or ckUSDC.

acceptanceCriteria: The Dashboard page shows a new diagnostics card/panel dedicated to token ledger configuration (in addition to the existing Transport Key Diagnostics).

For each of ckBTC, ckETH, ckUSDC, the panel shows: token symbol, configured ledger canister ID (or a clear “Not configured” state), and a status indicator for “Matches expected mainnet ID” vs “Mismatch”.
The panel provides a “Copy JSON” action that copies a JSON blob including timestamp and per-token fields (symbol, configuredLedgerCanisterId, expectedLedgerCanisterId, matchesExpected).
All user-facing text in this panel is in English.

requirements.text: Add a non-transfer connectivity/metadata check per token ledger canister to further confirm wiring without sending funds by calling read-only ICRC methods (e.g., name/symbol/decimals and/or supported standards) and surfacing success/failure in the Token Ledger Diagnostics UI.

quotes: check and confirm whether the correct mainnet ledger IDs confirm everything is wired up properly without sending any real ckBTC, ckETH, or ckUSDC.

acceptanceCriteria: The app performs only read-only calls for this verification (no transfer calls are made).
For each configured token, the diagnostics UI shows whether the metadata check succeeded and displays returned values (at minimum decimals) when available.

If a ledger call fails (e.g., due to network/agent/canister issues), the UI shows a non-crashing error state for that token and still allows copying diagnostics JSON including the error message/stringified failure.

constraints: Do not perform any token transfers as part of verification; diagnostics must use read-only ledger calls only.
Do not edit files under `frontend/src/components/ui` or any other paths listed as immutable in SYSTEM_CONTEXT.
nonGoals: Implementing real send/receive token transfer functionality.
Adding token balance fetching to the dashboard (unless explicitly requested later).
Changing backend Motoko logic or adding new backend actors/services.
